patch.value: Extension activation adds ~200ms to every command when bundled packages are installed

Measured 2026-07-04 (best-of-3, warm, node dist/cli.js): bare workspace list/get/create = 195-232ms; same commands with all 11 bundled packages installed = 361-461ms (health 569ms). pm --version = 52ms, so ~150ms is ESM import of the CLI itself and installed extensions roughly double per-command latency. Candidates: lazy per-command activation via activation.commands narrowing for built-ins that register global surfaces, module-graph precompute/cache for extension entries, or a persisted activation manifest so unaffected commands skip loading entries entirely.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Raw timing evidence (sandbox1, best-of-3): list 361ms, get 373ms, next 410ms, search 446ms, create 461ms, update 422ms, health 569ms, context 399ms vs bare workspace list 195ms, list-all 199ms, create 232ms; pm --version 52ms.

patch.value: Warm per-command latency with all bundled packages installed within 1.5x of bare workspace (measured via best-of-3 node dist/cli.js timings).

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: 2026-07-04 optimization landed (uncommitted): SDK entrypoints (sdk/index, runtime, testing) now bundle in the SAME esbuild invocation as the CLI with shared chunks; package.json exports point import/default at dist/cli-bundle/sdk*.js (types stay on dist/sdk/*.d.ts). Extensions importing @unbrained/pm-cli/sdk now reuse chunk modules the CLI already loaded — one shared copy of core state instead of two. Cold dist/sdk/index.js import was 226ms (138 local modules); bundled sdk.js import 79ms; shared-chunk variant near-free after CLI load. Warm best-of-N pm list with 11 packages: 651ms -> 331-385ms (bare 189-263ms). Remaining ~150ms = per-extension ESM resolution through 11 node_modules symlinks + 11 entry.ts type-strip imports + activation; next lever is activation.commands narrowing / persisted activation manifest.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Extension SDK imports resolved through the package exports to the unbundled dist/sdk graph while the CLI ran from its bundle — two copies of core, ~226ms extra ESM cost. Bundling SDK entrypoints in the same esbuild call as the CLI (entryPoints object: main/sdk/sdk-runtime/sdk-testing, splitting on) makes extensions share the CLI's already-loaded chunks. CPU profiles show source-mapped original filenames, so dist/sdk/*.js frames can appear even when only the bundle loads.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: 2026-07-04 full coverage gate green after change: 5338 tests, 100% statements/branches/functions/lines. Spec updates: release-readiness-runtime + package-manifest exports assertions moved to dist/cli-bundle/sdk*.js paths.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Activation-narrowing audit: extensionNeedsActivationForProbe already skips extensions whose declared activation.commands don't match. Always-activated today: pm-governance-audit (hooks), pm-lifecycle-hooks (hooks), pm-command-kit (parser) — genuinely global; pm-kanban declares only [schema] with NO activation.commands so it falls into the conservative tier and activates on EVERY command. Remaining lever is making activation itself cheap (per-package prebundled entry chunks or persisted activation manifest), not more narrowing.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: 2026-07-04 contention-free re-measurement from fresh 2026.7.4 tarball pack in CJS sandbox (best-of-5, warm): list 395ms / get 418ms / next 518ms with all 11 packages vs bare list 192ms; pm --version 63ms. Ratio ~2.06x — improved from pre-fix 651ms but acceptance (≤1.5x bare) not yet met. Item stays open; remaining lever per activation-narrowing audit is cheap activation (prebundled per-package entry chunks or persisted activation manifest) plus giving pm-kanban explicit activation.commands so it leaves the conservative always-activate tier.

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: Evidence: implemented command-scoped import filtering. loadExtensions now preserves full discovery but imports only extensions accepted by the CLI activation probe; CLI snapshot cache is scoped by activation probe; manifests with explicit activation.commands no longer load solely because they also declare hooks. Focused checks passed: node scripts/run-tests.mjs test -- tests/unit/extensions/extension-loader.spec.ts (107 tests), node scripts/run-tests.mjs test -- tests/unit/cli/cli-main-errors.spec.ts (119 tests), pnpm typecheck. Temp workspace with explicit install '*' installed 11 packages and improved profile list loaded extensions to 3 (governance-audit no longer always activates); shell best-of-7 still did not consistently meet the <=1.5x target (bare/list 174ms vs package/list 323ms after local lifecycle simulation), so item remains open for the next cheap-activation/persisted-manifest lever.

value.text: Evidence update: command-scoped activation filtering now preserves full discovery while filtering imports by activation probe, scopes runtime snapshot cache by normalized activation scope, avoids double activation for dynamic/template/search commands, and keeps hook/render-only extensions globally active without re-broadening command-bearing explicit activation manifests. Verification passed: node scripts/run-tests.mjs test -- tests/integration/extension-startup.integration.spec.ts; node scripts/run-tests.mjs test -- tests/unit/cli/cli-main-errors.spec.ts; node scripts/run-tests.mjs test -- tests/unit/extensions/extension-loader.spec.ts; pnpm typecheck; node scripts/run-tests.mjs coverage (263 files, 5337 tests, 100/100/100/100); pnpm lint; pnpm audit --audit-level moderate; pnpm security:scan; pnpm quality:docs-skills; node scripts/gen-package-runtime-loaders.mjs --check; pnpm changelog:pm:check; pm health --check-only --summary --json. Item remains open because the package-vs-bare latency acceptance target still needs another lever.

value.text: Review-response evidence: addressed Cubic/Gemini follow-ups by adding multi-extension activation/capability summary filters for package describe unions, scoping type-stripping runtime-loader fallback to the attempted runtime path, preserving list sort constant exports, and making ctx/context fallback parsing alias/flag aware. Verification passed: node scripts/run-tests.mjs test -- tests/unit/core/extensions/activation-summary.spec.ts tests/unit/extensions/extension-describe.spec.ts; node scripts/run-tests.mjs test -- tests/unit/packages/runtime-loaders.spec.ts tests/unit/scripts/gen-package-runtime-loaders.spec.ts; node scripts/run-tests.mjs test -- tests/unit/packages/runtime-loaders.spec.ts tests/unit/cli/error-guidance.spec.ts tests/unit/extensions/extension-describe.spec.ts tests/unit/core/extensions/activation-summary.spec.ts; pnpm typecheck; pnpm lint; git diff --check; full node scripts/run-tests.mjs coverage (263 files, 5353 tests, 100% statements/branches/functions/lines: 32922/32922, 23423/23423, 6470/6470, 31632/31632); node scripts/gen-package-runtime-loaders.mjs --check; pnpm changelog:pm && pnpm changelog:pm:check; pnpm audit --audit-level moderate; pnpm security:scan; pm health --check-only --summary --json. pm validate --check-resolution --check-history-drift has clean history drift and the pre-existing closed-item resolution metadata warning validate_resolution_missing_fields:267.
